tags.description: 83m long cave complex near harbour; divers can surface inside vast air chamber containing statue of patron saint San Bartolomeo; two communicating chambers; wall of astroides and sponges on approach

Remnant volcanic cone ~3 miles off NE coast; cap at 3m, vertical columnar basalt wall to 30m plateau, base at ~42m. Wreck of marble carrier MV ITA (sank Feb 21 2005) in two sections at 24-26m. Large barracuda schools, amberjack, groupers, tuna.

NW coast offshore rock; complex of caves, arches, canyons; Grotta della Balena (Whale Cave) with whale-mouth entrance ~20m, ~70m long. Dusky and striped groupers, amberjack, barracuda, hake, sea bream, spider crab, nudibranchs.

Southernmost point of Ustica; two large rocks rise from 40m sandy base; walls colonized by red gorgonians; tunnels and passages lead to gorgonian forest at depth; lobsters, barracuda, brown and red groupers, nudibranchs, orange madrepore (Astroides calycularis)
Blue Grotto near harbour; large room ~30m diameter; connects via tunnel to Grotta di San Francesco; light plays on sponge-encrusted walls; suitable for all certification levels and courses
Underwater archaeological itinerary near Punta Gavazzi lighthouse; lead anchors III century BC to IV century AD and amphora fragments at up to 20m depth. Black lava volcanic seabed.
NW tip of Ustica near Spalmatore tower; Zone A boundary (strict no-take zone); shallow black volcanic rock slopes; groupers, wrasse, sea bream; excellent shore entry for photography and first dives
Pyroclastic rock wall beneath 16th-century lighthouse; gorgonians from 20m on squared blocks, resident groupers, passing amberjack schools; cavern with conger eels; Zones B/C boundary of MPA
Sheltered cove in southern Ustica within Zone A of MPA; black pebble beach; shallow volcanic reef to 10m; groupers, wrasse, damselfish habituated to divers; good night dive site

Deep technical pinnacle ~2 miles NW of Ustica; cap at 60m depth (not accessible to recreational divers); NW side plummets to 80+ meters. Extremely challenging — requires technical diving certification and DPV. Not suitable for recreational diving.

tags.name: Le Piramidi
tags.description: Pyramid-shaped volcanic boulders on W coast near Punta Spalmatore; top at 5-10m; Grotta dei Cirri cave at ~28-48m with panoramic openings; Plesionika narval (unicorn) shrimps, dusky groupers, lobsters, meagre

tags.name: Sutta zà Lisa
tags.description: Basaltic lava rock wall to 20m; amphora fragments in lava substrate; dusky groupers, octopus; photographic interest; archaeological character within Ustica MPA

tags.name: Grotta dei Gamberi
tags.description: Punta Galera cave; elliptical entry arch at ~40m; main chamber ~50m wide; exits at 24-28m. Walls covered in thousands of Plesionika narval (unicorn shrimps). Nudibranchs (Facelina coronata, Peltodoris atromaculata), Pinna nobilis, conger eels, lobsters, forkbeard.
